automotive-ev-battery.title: BYD's luxury SUV received a record 150,000 orders, and now it's headed overseas
automotive-ev-battery.summary: BYD's flagship luxury SUV, the Great Tang, has achieved record pre-launch orders of 150,000 units in China and is now expanding internationally, including to Europe by the end of 2026. This expansion marks a significant milestone for the Chinese automaker's premium vehicle segment in global markets.

automotive-ev-battery.title: BYD Datang Comparison Reveals Accelerating EV Progress
automotive-ev-battery.summary: BYD's newly launched Datang premium SUV demonstrates significant technological advancement in the Chinese EV market, with rear-wheel-drive models starting at $35,502 USD and offering up to 950 km of class-leading range. The vehicle's competitive pricing and performance benchmarks reflect rapid acceleration in EV development across China's automotive industry.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Uruguay EV Sales Report: ICEV Sales Start Melting Down as BEVs Surpass 40% Share in May
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Uruguay has emerged as Latin America's EV sales leader, achieving a 40% battery electric vehicle market share in May 2026 and reaching 20% for the full year 2025, up dramatically from 8.5% in 2024. This exceptional growth trajectory demonstrates one of the world's most aggressive EV adoption rates outside traditional markets.

automotive-ev-battery.title: India to slash tariffs on UK-made cars starting in July
automotive-ev-battery.summary: India and the UK are implementing their Free Trade Agreement on July 15, which will reduce tariffs on UK-manufactured vehicles including internal combustion engines, hybrids, electric, and hydrogen vehicles. This trade agreement aims to make British-built automotive imports significantly more competitive in the Indian market.

automotive-ev-battery.title: 400 hp Mercedes-Benz VLE 300 combines limo luxury with lots of legroom
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Mercedes-Benz has unveiled the VLE 300 electric MPV, featuring 800V battery technology, limousine-level luxury amenities, seating for eight, and exceptional rear legroom. This vehicle represents the premium segment's push toward electric multipurpose vehicles with enhanced passenger comfort and advanced battery infrastructure.

automotive-ev-battery.title: India: UP deploys 45 e-buses, 3 hydrogen buses for public transport
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Uttar Pradesh state in India added 45 electric buses and three hydrogen buses to its public transportation fleet on June 12, while simultaneously inaugurating a new electric bus depot in Noida. This deployment represents significant progress in India's transition toward zero-emission public transportation infrastructure.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Bosch eBike Systems launches first hub motor for e-bikes
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Bosch eBike Systems has introduced its first hub motor, the Hub Line, expanding beyond its established mid-drive motor technology and concurrently launching the PowerTube 360, its slimmest battery design to date. This product expansion diversifies Bosch's e-mobility portfolio and addresses different e-bike segment requirements.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Hyundai is giving the Kona EV a complete overhaul, and it already looks like an upgrade
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Hyundai is introducing a comprehensively redesigned Kona EV featuring updated styling and the latest infotainment technology. The vehicle refresh positions Hyundai's popular electric crossover to maintain competitiveness in the increasingly crowded EV market segment.

automotive-ev-battery.title: Yup, China's EV Price War Was Brutal, But It Drove Innovation
automotive-ev-battery.summary: China's competitive EV price war, despite creating short-term market disruption, has accelerated innovation and technological advancement across the industry. The analysis reveals that industry consolidation and competitive pressures have paradoxically strengthened the sector's innovative capacity and market dynamism.

automotive-ev-battery.title: As Kenya's Electric Motorcycle Ecosystem Grows, Local Firms Are Starting to Specialise and Forge Interesting Partnerships With Large Global Firms
automotive-ev-battery.summary: Kenya's electric motorcycle sector is experiencing significant growth as local manufacturers transition from initial product validation to full-scale commercialization, increasingly partnering with global firms while specializing in different market segments. This ecosystem development demonstrates emerging market innovation in EV technology adapted for regional mobility needs.

world-news.title: US-Iran talks to begin in Switzerland as Tehran says it closed Strait of Hormuz
world-news.summary: Diplomatic negotiations between the US and Iran are set to commence in Switzerland amid escalating tensions, with Tehran claiming it has closed the strategically critical Strait of Hormuz in response to Israeli military operations in Lebanon. The US has disputed Iran's assertion regarding the waterway's closure, marking a critical moment in regional geopolitics.

world-news.title: Iran war live: Vance heads to Switzerland; Israel kills 16 in Lebanon
world-news.summary: US Vice President Vance is traveling to Switzerland for peace negotiations as Israeli military operations in Lebanon continue, with reports indicating 16 casualties in recent strikes. The talks represent a critical diplomatic effort amid ongoing regional conflict and Iranian threats to close the Strait of Hormuz.

world-news.title: Trump vows Iran will not charge Strait of Hormuz tolls, but says US might
world-news.summary: President Trump has pledged that Iran will not impose tolls on the Strait of Hormuz, while indicating the US may do so in the future under a new memorandum of understanding. The agreement allows for an initial 60-day toll-free period but does not preclude future US charges on global shipping.

world-news.title: Ukraine says Russian attack kills five people in Zaporizhzhia
world-news.summary: Russian military attacks have claimed five lives in Ukraine's Zaporizhzhia region, according to Ukrainian officials, as President Zelenskyy warned of preparations for a large-scale offensive. The incident underscores the ongoing intensity of the Russia-Ukraine conflict.

world-news.title: Bolivian president declares state of emergency
world-news.summary: Bolivia's president has declared a state of emergency to address weeks of anti-government protests that have disrupted supply chains and created acute shortages of essential goods. The declaration reflects deepening political instability in the nation.

world-news.title: Australia confirms first case of H5N1 bird flu as virus reaches every continent
world-news.summary: Australia has confirmed its first case of the H5N1 bird flu strain, making it the final continent to detect the virus and marking a significant escalation in the global spread of the disease. The development has major implications for international biosecurity and pandemic preparedness.

world-news.title: Zelensky returns highest Polish honour after award stripped
world-news.summary: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy has returned Poland's highest national honor after it was rescinded, while signaling openness to diplomatic engagement regarding contested historical narratives between the two nations. The gesture represents efforts to resolve diplomatic tensions between the neighboring countries.

world-news.title: African and Caribbean nations call for formal apology for transatlantic slavery
world-news.summary: Leaders from African and Caribbean nations have formally called for apologies, debt relief, and financial reparations from countries that profited from the transatlantic slave trade. The collective demand represents a significant push for historical accountability on the global stage.

world-news.title: Israeli strikes kill six people in Gaza including Al Jazeera cameraman, officials say
world-news.summary: Israeli military operations in Gaza have resulted in six deaths, including an Al Jazeera cameraman, with the Israeli military claiming the journalist was a Hamas operative without providing corroborating evidence. The strike raises ongoing concerns about press freedom and civilian casualties in the conflict.

world-news.title: Meloni tells Trump to 'focus on your own popularity' as row escalates
world-news.summary: Italian Prime Minister Meloni has publicly rebuked US President Trump over remarks questioning her popularity and suggesting she sought special attention at a G7 summit, escalating diplomatic tensions between the two leaders. The exchange reflects broader friction within Western alliance relations.

science-space.title: Supermassive black holes may be surrounded by dark matter clusters, new 'echo map' technique suggests
science-space.summary: A novel echo mapping technique reveals that supermassive black holes, including the one at the center of the Milky Way, may be surrounded by clusters of dark matter. This discovery provides new insights into the composition and structure of regions around these cosmic objects.

science-space.title: We've found a mysterious substance on Titan and Pluto
science-space.summary: Researchers have identified a mysterious substance absorbing light on the surfaces of Pluto and Saturn's moon Titan, a discovery that could be critical to understanding the complex chemistry of these distant worlds. Characterizing this material may reveal important information about the composition and geological processes of these celestial bodies.

science-space.title: This simple twist could bring quantum computers closer to reality
science-space.summary: Researchers have demonstrated that twisting layered sheets of hexagonal boron nitride can significantly alter the light produced by embedded quantum emitters, offering unprecedented control over components essential for quantum computing. This technique represents a breakthrough in controlling the fundamental building blocks needed for future quantum computers, communications systems, and sensors.

science-space.title: A quantum state that lasts forever may finally be within our grasp
science-space.summary: Experimental evidence suggests that a quantum state that remains frozen indefinitely—previously thought impossible under thermodynamic laws—may now be achievable. This breakthrough could enable the creation of entirely new types of matter with transformative applications across physics and materials science.

science-space.title: Do we need a lunar building code to build moon bases safely?
science-space.summary: As NASA advances its plans for sustained human presence on the moon, an expert argues that the development of a comprehensive lunar building code is essential to ensure safety in construction projects. Establishing standardized building protocols would address the unique environmental challenges and hazards specific to lunar operations.

science-space.title: This DNA repair gene went rogue and exposed a cancer weakness
science-space.summary: Scientists have identified a paradoxical mechanism where EXO1, a gene normally protective against DNA damage, becomes dangerous when overproduced and begins cutting DNA indiscriminately. This discovery reveals a previously unknown cancer vulnerability that could inform new therapeutic strategies.

science-space.title: Faecal transplant makes the brains of old mice act young again
science-space.summary: A groundbreaking study found that older mice receiving fecal microbiome transplants from younger animals exhibited improved brain plasticity and enhanced neurological function. This finding suggests that microbiome transplantation could reverse age-related cognitive decline and overcome neurological conditions typically treatable only in childhood.

science-space.title: Scientists found a cannabis compound that relieves pain without the high
science-space.summary: Research demonstrates that cannabis-derived terpenes, particularly geraniol, significantly reduce chronic pain in animal models of fibromyalgia and post-surgical pain without psychoactive effects. This discovery offers a promising non-intoxicating alternative for pain management and could expand therapeutic applications of cannabis compounds.

science-space.title: A single cobalt shock could trigger global EV battery supply chaos
science-space.summary: Research reveals that the global cobalt supply chain for electric vehicle batteries is far more interconnected and vulnerable than previously understood, with localized disruptions capable of cascading into international crises. Protecting battery supply chains will require comprehensive system-wide coordination and risk management across multiple countries and industries.

science-space.title: Hidden geological process offsets carbon emissions from thawing permafrost
science-space.summary: Scientists have discovered that increased rock weathering triggered by thawing permafrost absorbs carbon dioxide from the atmosphere at rates sufficient to offset—and in some regions, surpass—greenhouse gas emissions from river discharge. This natural carbon sequestration process could significantly impact global carbon cycle calculations and climate modeling.

finance-markets.title: Iran says it closed Strait of Hormuz after Israel and Hizbollah exchange fire
finance-markets.summary: Iran's military command announced the closure of the Strait of Hormuz in response to ongoing Israeli operations in Lebanon, creating potential disruption to global energy markets and shipping. The announcement coincides with U.S. Vice President Harris heading to Switzerland for peace negotiations with Iranian representatives, raising questions about the viability of diplomatic efforts.

finance-markets.title: Fed's Favorite Gauge Is Seen Showing Faster Inflation
finance-markets.summary: The Federal Reserve's preferred inflation measure is expected to show an acceleration, potentially reinforcing the central bank's consensus around the need for interest-rate increases in the coming year. This development could have significant implications for monetary policy decisions and asset valuations across markets.

finance-markets.title: A Stock Trader's Guide to Navigating a Rare 'Super El Niño'
finance-markets.summary: As geopolitical tensions ease, investors are increasingly focused on climate risk from a rare 'Super El Niño' event, prompting reassessment of portfolio positioning across agriculture, insurance, and other climate-sensitive sectors. The shift highlights how environmental factors are becoming material considerations for financial market strategy.

finance-markets.title: South Africa's Kganyago Warns Inflation Expectations Are Rising
finance-markets.summary: South Africa's central bank governor has signaled early indicators of second-round inflation effects as underlying price pressures mount, indicating the need for proactive policy measures. The warning reflects growing concerns about wage-price spirals and expectations-anchoring challenges across emerging markets.

finance-markets.title: Brazil's Lula Holds Lead Against Bolsonaro After Bank Scandal
finance-markets.summary: President Luiz Inacio Lula da Silva maintains his polling advantage over Senator Flavio Bolsonaro ahead of the Brazilian presidential election, as Bolsonaro's political standing is undermined by associations with a major banking fraud scandal. The outcome of Brazil's electoral contest carries implications for regional economic policy and investor confidence.

finance-markets.title: Iran Sanctions Losing Efficacy
finance-markets.summary: While sanctions were instrumental in bringing Iran to negotiations in 2015, military conflict has now become the primary mechanism of influence, with experts warning that businesses may hesitate to re-engage with Iran even if sanctions are lifted due to persistent geopolitical risks. This shift reflects the diminishing returns of financial sanctions as a policy tool in escalating regional conflicts.

finance-markets.title: Iran reportedly closes Strait of Hormuz again as Vance heads to Switzerland for talks
finance-markets.summary: Iran's joint military command announced a second closure of the Strait of Hormuz in response to continued Israeli military operations in Lebanon, creating fresh uncertainty for global oil markets and shipping routes. The action complicates ongoing diplomatic negotiations and signals escalating regional tensions.

finance-markets.title: Rahm Emanuel says Trump 'got schooled' by Iran in bad ceasefire deal
finance-markets.summary: A potential Democratic presidential candidate has criticized recent ceasefire negotiations involving Iran as a strategic misstep for the United States, characterizing the situation as an 'American national security mess' with lasting implications. The comments reflect growing political divisions over Middle East policy and diplomatic strategy.

finance-markets.title: Did Anthropic talk its way into an AI export ban?
finance-markets.summary: Financial Times analysis indicates that Anthropic has issued substantially more public warnings about advanced AI risks compared to rival OpenAI, potentially influencing regulatory scrutiny and export controls on artificial intelligence technology. The company's communications strategy may have inadvertently contributed to policy measures that restrict the sector's commercial operations.

finance-markets.title: Pedro Sánchez's wife ordered to face corruption trial and surrender passport
finance-markets.summary: A Spanish judge has ordered the wife of Prime Minister Pedro Sánchez to stand trial on corruption charges and surrender her passport, prompting accusations of judicial overreach from the prime minister's office. The case raises governance concerns in a major EU economy and could affect market confidence in Spanish political stability.

tech-ai.title: Nobel laureate John Jumper is leaving DeepMind for rival Anthropic
tech-ai.summary: Nobel Prize-winning scientist John Jumper is departing Google DeepMind to join Anthropic, marking a significant talent shift in the competitive AI industry. The move represents a broader pattern of senior researchers leaving DeepMind for rival AI companies.

tech-ai.title: NASA selects Eric Schmidt's rocket company for a 2028 mission to Mars
tech-ai.summary: NASA has selected Relativity Space, led by former Google executive Eric Schmidt, to launch the Aeolus payload to Mars in 2028 under a public-private partnership. The mission will provide the first integrated, daily global view of Mars's atmosphere.

tech-ai.title: The Atlantic created a searchable database of the music used to train AI
tech-ai.summary: Reporter Alex Reisner has uncovered and made searchable four datasets of music being used to train AI models, containing between 100,000 and 12 million tracks each. The discovery raises questions about which companies, potentially including Google and Stability AI, have utilized this training data.

tech-ai.title: From PGP to Mythos: a brief history of export controls that didn't stop anyone
tech-ai.summary: A historical analysis reveals that three decades of cybersecurity software export controls have proven ineffective, raising doubts about the efficacy of similar restrictions on Anthropic's new Mythos cybersecurity model. The article examines why such regulatory approaches have consistently failed to restrict information flow.

tech-ai.title: Signal's Meredith Whittaker wants you to remember that AI chatbots 'are not your friends'
tech-ai.summary: Signal president Meredith Whittaker cautions the public against anthropomorphizing AI chatbots, emphasizing they are not conscious beings or sentient entities despite their conversational abilities. The warning addresses growing concerns about users forming misleading relationships with AI systems.

tech-ai.title: The UK will scan asylum-seekers' faces for age checks—despite knowing the tech is flawed
tech-ai.summary: The United Kingdom plans to implement facial recognition technology for age verification of asylum seekers despite evidence that such systems produce significant and life-altering errors. Tests of the age-verification technology have revealed substantial reliability concerns.

tech-ai.title: Go eyes robotaxis and acquisitions after Japan's biggest IPO of 2026 — here's why it matters
tech-ai.summary: Japan's largest IPO of 2026 has provided ride-hailing app Go with significant capital to address the country's critical driver shortage through robotaxi development and strategic acquisitions. The public offering represents a major boost to Japan's languishing listing market.

tech-ai.title: Nothing cancels this year's CMF phone due to RAM prices
tech-ai.summary: Nothing has canceled its planned successor to the CMF Phone 2 Pro due to elevated memory prices that would prevent delivering a meaningful upgrade at a competitive price point. The decision reflects broader industry challenges amid what the company describes as critical RAM pricing pressures.

tech-ai.title: Every new iOS 27 feature that's worth knowing about
tech-ai.summary: Apple's iOS 27 update includes numerous practical features beyond the headline-grabbing Siri AI and Apple Intelligence enhancements. The update addresses various functionality improvements across the operating system.

tech-ai.title: Hue's wired wall modules bring non-smart lights into its ecosystem
tech-ai.summary: Philips Hue has launched its first wired wall modules designed to integrate existing non-smart lighting into the Hue ecosystem when installed behind conventional switches. The company also introduced more affordable Play lamps and upgraded E14 candle bulbs with expanded white light spectrum and Matter-over-Thread support.

cybersecurity.title: CISA Admin Leaked AWS GovCloud Keys on Github
cybersecurity.summary: A contractor for the Cybersecurity & Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) publicly exposed highly privileged AWS GovCloud credentials and internal system details on GitHub until recently. Security experts characterized the incident as one of the most serious government data leaks in recent history, revealing sensitive information about CISA's software build, testing, and deployment processes.

cybersecurity.title: Microsoft Links Mastra AI Supply Chain Attack to North Korean Hackers
cybersecurity.summary: Microsoft has attributed a Mastra AI supply chain attack affecting over 140 npm packages to the North Korean threat group Sapphire Sleet, also known as BlueNoroff. The incident represents a significant supply chain compromise targeting developers and organizations relying on compromised software dependencies.

cybersecurity.title: Texas Govt Data Breach Exposes Over 3 Million Driver's Licenses
cybersecurity.summary: The Texas Parks and Wildlife Department disclosed a data breach at a license system vendor that compromised personal information for more than three million individuals. The incident highlights vulnerabilities in third-party vendor relationships managing sensitive government identification data.

cybersecurity.title: Sweeping Credential-Harvesting Heist Compromises 30K+ Fortinet Devices
cybersecurity.summary: Attackers have compiled working credentials for tens of thousands of compromised Fortinet devices across nearly 200 countries and multiple business sectors. The widespread credential harvesting campaign demonstrates systematic compromise of enterprise security infrastructure globally.

cybersecurity.title: Alleged Kimwolf Botmaster 'Dort' Arrested, Charged in U.S. and Canada
cybersecurity.summary: Canadian authorities arrested a 23-year-old Ottawa man suspected of operating Kimwolf, an IoT botnet that infected millions of devices and launched massive distributed denial-of-service attacks over six months. The suspect previously targeted security researchers and journalists with DDoS, doxing, and swatting campaigns.

cybersecurity.title: Klue OAuth Breach Victim List Grows as Icarus Hackers Claim Attack
cybersecurity.summary: Market intelligence platform Klue confirmed a security breach allowing threat actors to steal OAuth tokens used to connect to customers' Salesforce environments, with the newly emerged "Icarus" extortion group publicly claiming responsibility. The attack exposes the risks of compromised OAuth authentication across enterprise integrations.

cybersecurity.title: Hackers Exploit Gravity SMTP WordPress Plugin Bug to Expose API Keys
cybersecurity.summary: Threat actors are actively exploiting a patched vulnerability (CVE-2026-4020) in the Gravity SMTP WordPress plugin, installed on approximately 100,000 websites, to extract sensitive data including API keys, secrets, and OAuth tokens. The medium-severity information disclosure flaw allows unauthenticated attackers to access configuration credentials.

cybersecurity.title: Novo Nordisk Breach Exposes Software Development Pipeline Risk
cybersecurity.summary: A leaked GitHub token from Novo Nordisk illustrates a critical organizational failure in secrets management, highlighting the need to treat credential protection as an identity and access control problem rather than merely a tooling issue. The incident underscores risks within software development pipelines when authentication tokens are inadequately secured.

cybersecurity.title: Patch Tuesday, May 2026 Edition
cybersecurity.summary: Major software vendors including Apple, Google, Microsoft, Mozilla, and Oracle released near-record volumes of security patches this month, demonstrating that artificial intelligence tools are proving effective at discovering vulnerabilities in human-written code. The increased patch velocity reflects both the growing attack surface and AI's capability to identify previously unknown security flaws.

cybersecurity.title: INC Ransomware Thrives by Mastering the Basics
cybersecurity.summary: The INC ransomware operation demonstrates how threat actors achieve sustained success by focusing on high-impact sectors such as healthcare, where operational disruption creates immediate financial pressure to pay ransoms. The group's effectiveness underscores the ongoing threat posed by disciplined ransomware campaigns targeting critical infrastructure.

startups-vc.title: NEA's Tiffany Luck on AI IPOs, personal agents, and the ROI reckoning
startups-vc.summary: NEA partner Tiffany Luck discusses the current AI investment landscape as companies face a reckoning with inflated AI spending and budgets. The article explores the shift from aggressive AI tokenmaxxing by tech leaders to more measured adoption as organizations confront the actual returns on their artificial intelligence investments.

startups-vc.title: I Sold My AI Startup Before Revenue: Here's What Investors Missed — And Founders Shouldn't
startups-vc.summary: Angel investor Alexander Kardos-Nyheim argues that long-term AI value will accrue to companies solving deep technical challenges at the model and infrastructure level rather than application-layer products. The commentary provides frameworks for evaluating AI startup investability and shares lessons from the author's own exit experience.

startups-vc.title: 'This System Wasn't Built For Me': Black Founders Became Investors To Change Venture Capital
startups-vc.summary: Black founders facing persistent funding gaps in venture capital have turned to investing to address systemic inequities in the industry. The article profiles founders-turned-investors and examines how their experiences in capital raising inform their approach to backing underrepresented entrepreneurs.

startups-vc.title: Meesho Goes Public
startups-vc.summary: Meesho, a Y Combinator S16 company, completed its IPO today, establishing itself as one of India's largest e-commerce platforms serving millions of small merchants. The milestone represents a decade of growth and user-focused building by co-founders Vidit and Sanjeev.

startups-vc.title: BillionToOne Goes Public — The Startup That Made Genetic Testing Universal
startups-vc.summary: BillionToOne went public today as Y Combinator's fourth publicly traded biotech company, having democratized genetic testing in the U.S. with approximately one in eleven babies born in the country now tested using their fetal genetic test.

startups-vc.title: Congratulations to Groww
startups-vc.summary: Groww, India's leading digital investing platform, completed its IPO today, marking Y Combinator's first company to achieve a public listing on Indian exchanges. The platform serves tens of millions of users for stock, mutual fund, and ETF investing.

startups-vc.title: Diana Hu Is YC's Newest Managing Partner
startups-vc.summary: Y Combinator announced Diana Hu's promotion to Managing Partner, bringing experience from both founding a startup and shipping technology to over 100 million users. Her appointment strengthens YC's leadership team with operational and product expertise.

startups-vc.title: Harshita Arora Joins YC as General Partner
startups-vc.summary: Y Combinator hired Harshita Arora as General Partner, leveraging her deep expertise in fintech and infrastructure built through founding companies since her teenage years. Her addition brings a founder's product instinct to the accelerator's partnership team.

startups-vc.title: 'Queer Eye' life coach Karamo Brown launches Kē, a wellness app featuring his AI digital clone
startups-vc.summary: Karamo Brown launched Kē, a wellness application featuring an AI digital clone of himself, after spending 18 months on his own personal development journey. The app aims to help users across fitness, nutrition, meditation, sobriety, relationships, and personal growth.

startups-vc.title: Pixi's new iOS app turns text messages into interactive AR experiences
startups-vc.summary: Pixi launched an iOS application that transforms text messaging by enabling interactive augmented reality experiences, positioning itself as the next evolution beyond traditional stickers, GIFs, and emoji reactions. The startup is betting on AR-enriched messaging as the future of mobile communication.

crypto-web3.title: How STRC lost its par: The timeline behind Strategy's preferred-stock meltdown
crypto-web3.summary: Strategy's STRC preferred stock experienced a significant collapse from par value due to a combination of factors including bond buybacks, depleted cash reserves, and bearish bitcoin market conditions. The incident has sparked broader market concerns about the sustainability of dividend-paying crypto instruments and institutional risk management practices.

crypto-web3.title: Secret Network's Axelar bridge drained for $4.67 million in infinite-mint exploit that went unnoticed for seven days
crypto-web3.summary: An attacker exploited an infinite-mint vulnerability in Secret Network's Axelar bridge to drain approximately $4.67 million, with the exploit remaining undetected for a week. Of the stolen funds, $770,000 remains in the attacker's wallet after Axelar declined Secret Network's request to freeze the assets.

crypto-web3.title: 'Most difficult day in the history of digital credit': Strive CEO says leverage liquidation drove STRC and SATA selloff
crypto-web3.summary: Strive's CEO attributed the sharp decline of STRC and SATA preferred stocks to the unwinding of leveraged positions that pushed both instruments significantly below their $100 par values. The incident highlights systemic risks in the leverage-dependent crypto finance ecosystem and raises questions about margin management across institutional platforms.

crypto-web3.title: Schwab to join prediction markets race with S&P 500 event-based options: WSJ
crypto-web3.summary: Charles Schwab is entering the prediction market sector by developing S&P 500 binary options contracts in partnership with Cboe, marking the major brokerage's first foray into event-based trading products. The move reflects growing institutional interest in prediction markets, a space currently dominated by platforms like Kalshi and Polymarket.

crypto-web3.title: AI is making crypto security cheaper, faster and harder to ignore
crypto-web3.summary: Researchers indicate that increasingly affordable and accessible AI-powered security tools are poised to reshape industry standards for code deployment due diligence across the crypto sector. The technology could fundamentally alter expectations for how developers and institutions approach smart contract security and risk management.

crypto-web3.title: Live markets: Bitcoin under pressure as Saylor comments on STRC selloff
crypto-web3.summary: Approximately 20 percent of bitcoin miners have become unprofitable amid recent price weakness, forcing publicly traded mining operators to liquidate over 32,000 bitcoin in the first quarter—exceeding their entire 2025 offloadings. The mining pressure continues to weigh on bitcoin prices as the network struggles with operational sustainability.

crypto-web3.title: What Is the CLARITY Act? The Crypto Market Structure Bill Explained
crypto-web3.summary: The CLARITY Act (Digital Asset Market Clarity Act) is proposed U.S. legislation that would establish a comprehensive federal regulatory framework governing how digital assets are issued, traded, and regulated. The bill aims to clarify which regulators oversee crypto firms and define compliance requirements across the industry.

crypto-web3.title: Franklin Templeton files for ETFs that reinvest stock dividends into bitcoin
crypto-web3.summary: Franklin Templeton has filed applications for two new exchange-traded funds designed to automatically reinvest stock dividend payments into bitcoin, with an expected launch date of September 1, 2026. The offerings represent an innovative approach to integrating traditional equities with cryptocurrency asset accumulation.

crypto-web3.title: Andre Cronje, two others resign from Sonic Labs board as S token trades 97% below its peak
crypto-web3.summary: Three board members, including prominent developer Andre Cronje, have resigned from Sonic Labs as the network's S token has plummeted 97 percent from its peak value. The departing executives stated they remain invested in the Layer 1 network's future but will no longer participate in business decision-making.

crypto-web3.title: House Republican Introduces Insider Trading Bill to Ban Lawmaker Prediction Market Bets
crypto-web3.summary: A House Republican has introduced legislation designed to prevent members of Congress and their family members from participating in prediction market bets related to policy outcomes. The bill aims to close a regulatory loophole that could enable lawmakers to profit from insider information on legislative matters.

health-biotech.title: FDA advisory panel endorses Moderna mRNA flu vaccine that was subject of controversy
health-biotech.summary: An FDA advisory committee unanimously endorsed Moderna's seasonal mRNA flu vaccine, marking a significant regulatory reversal after the agency declined to review the product earlier this year. The endorsement positions Moderna for potential approval and demonstrates a dramatic turnaround for the controversial vaccine candidate.

health-biotech.title: UniQure to file gene therapy for approval, reflecting major shifts at FDA
health-biotech.summary: UniQure's decision to file for regulatory approval of a gene therapy reflects significant policy shifts at the FDA following the departures of senior leadership officials. The development suggests current FDA leadership may adopt a more flexible approach to gene therapy approvals in certain cases.

health-biotech.title: Jazz zeroes in on next-gen T cell engagers with AbCellera deal
health-biotech.summary: Jazz Pharmaceuticals entered into a potentially $4 billion collaboration with AbCellera to develop next-generation T cell engagers, broadening the therapeutic applications of this drug class beyond blood cancers. The partnership reflects industry efforts to expand the limited success profile of T cell engagers into new disease areas.

health-biotech.title: Tennessee pharmacies sell potent ivermectin, led by anti-vaccine doctor who's taken 'bucketloads'
health-biotech.summary: Tennessee pharmacies are dispensing potent formulations of ivermectin under the direction of an anti-vaccine physician, raising concerns about the promotion of unproven treatments. The report highlights ongoing regulatory challenges surrounding the sale of antiparasitic drugs outside their approved clinical indications.

health-biotech.title: Eli Lilly ends 340B drug discounts to some hospitals for failing to provide claims data
health-biotech.summary: Eli Lilly has begun revoking mandatory drug discounts to a limited number of hospitals participating in the federal 340B program due to non-compliance with claims data reporting requirements. The action follows through on previous threats and signals increased pharmaceutical industry enforcement of program obligations.

health-biotech.title: Novo Nordisk expands in China and Czech; Cellares boosts Series D by $50M
health-biotech.summary: Novo Nordisk announced a $380 million investment to expand manufacturing capacity at its facility in the Czech Republic while simultaneously strengthening its presence in China. The company's expansion strategy reflects growing global demand for its pharmaceutical products and commitment to regional manufacturing infrastructure.

health-biotech.title: Kardigan extends biotech's streak of big IPOs with $400M haul
health-biotech.summary: Kardigan, a heart drug developer, completed a $400 million initial public offering, marking the fourth biotech company to exceed this threshold in 2026. The achievement represents the strongest year for large biotech IPOs since 2021, signaling renewed investor confidence in the sector.

health-biotech.title: Maternity deserts aren't accidents. They're the result of a design flaw
health-biotech.summary: Nearly half of U.S. counties lack practicing obstetricians or gynecologists, creating significant gaps in maternal healthcare access despite declining fertility rates. The analysis reveals that maternity deserts are systemic failures rather than geographic accidents, requiring structural healthcare policy reforms.

health-biotech.title: A new Westlake-backed biotech takes aim at migraines
health-biotech.summary: Vedana Therapeutics emerged from stealth with $46 million in funding to develop anti-PACAP therapies for migraine prevention, joining a growing number of companies pursuing this therapeutic strategy. The funding and launch reflect increasing commercial interest in novel approaches to address the substantial migraine market.

health-biotech.title: CMS recalculates Medicare Advantage star ratings again, adding more volatility to program
health-biotech.summary: The Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services has recalculated Medicare Advantage star ratings, introducing additional volatility into a program where bonus payments have reached $16 billion annually. The regulatory shift affects plan performance assessments and raises questions about the stability of the rating system.

energy-climate.title: New York completes 1.25GW CHPE power transmission line
energy-climate.summary: New York Governor Kathy Hochul announced completion of the 1,250MW Champlain Hudson Power Express (CHPE) high-voltage direct current transmission line, which is now capable of delivering clean hydroelectric power from Canada to New York City. The project represents a significant infrastructure advancement for regional decarbonization and grid reliability.

energy-climate.title: Trump administration buys out 4 more offshore wind leases for $765M
energy-climate.summary: The Trump administration has acquired four additional offshore wind leases for $765 million, with developer Invenergy redirecting the funds toward natural gas plants across the Midwest and geothermal projects. The buyout continues a pattern of policy shifts away from offshore wind development.

energy-climate.title: China Briefing 11 June 2026: Tech clampdown | Extreme weather | Provinces' energy plans
energy-climate.summary: Carbon Brief's China Briefing examines critical climate and energy policy developments in China, including technological regulations, extreme weather impacts, and provincial renewable energy expansion plans. The briefing provides comprehensive analysis of Asia's largest economy's energy transition strategies.

energy-climate.title: Ohmium, Hynfra partner on green hydrogen in Middle East and Africa
energy-climate.summary: Ohmium and Hynfra have established a master cooperation agreement to develop green hydrogen projects in Mauritania, Jordan, and Oman, expanding clean fuel production capacity in strategically important regions. The partnership addresses growing demand for hydrogen in industrial decarbonization across the Middle East and North Africa.

energy-climate.title: BESS as a 'Swiss Army knife' for data centers
energy-climate.summary: Battery energy storage systems are proving essential for managing rapid electricity demand fluctuations in artificial intelligence data centers, which introduce operational challenges not addressed by conventional power infrastructure. BESS deployment enables better grid stability while supporting the expanding computational demands of AI workloads.

energy-climate.title: Managed, bidirectional EV charging advances with utility, automaker support
energy-climate.summary: General Motors and Rivian's recent announcements demonstrate growing industry support for managed, bidirectional electric vehicle charging technology that can optimize grid load management. The expanding U.S. EV fleet's integration with grid infrastructure represents a significant opportunity for demand response and energy storage.

energy-climate.title: Phelan licenses Johnson Matthey tech for Saldanha Bay eSAF plant
energy-climate.summary: Phelan Green Hydrogen has licensed advanced technologies from Johnson Matthey for a proposed electrolytic sustainable aviation fuel production facility in South Africa's Western Cape. The project advances clean fuel alternatives for the aviation sector in Africa's renewable energy hub.

energy-climate.title: Eos receives first purchase order from FPUSA for Texas BESS project
energy-climate.summary: Eos Energy has received its first purchase order from Frontier Power USA Parent under a 2GWh capacity reservation agreement, marking commercial traction for the battery storage developer in the competitive Texas market. The order demonstrates increasing deployment momentum for alternative battery technologies in utility-scale applications.

energy-climate.title: Heat pump shipments rise through April, with more use for both heating and cooling
energy-climate.summary: U.S. heat pump shipments continued to increase through April despite ongoing legal challenges to gas water heater and furnace regulations, indicating sustained market growth in residential heating and cooling electrification. The data suggests consumer demand remains robust for efficient heating solutions.

energy-climate.title: Fewer storms, not less risk: El Niño will bring mixed results across US power systems
energy-climate.summary: El Niño conditions are expected to reduce Atlantic hurricane activity but create localized power system vulnerabilities through shifting flood and wildfire patterns across different U.S. regions. Utilities must prepare for regionally variable climate impacts that present complex operational challenges beyond traditional storm mitigation.

asia-pacific.title: Italy’s Meloni let Trump’s jibes slide for so long. Now, she’s hitting back
asia-pacific.summary:
US Donald Trump has sparred with most of his fellow Group of Seven leaders at some point. But Italy’s Giorgia Meloni this week did something none of them dared: she escalated.
Unlike Spanish Socialist Pedro Sanchez, one of the few leaders in Europe who has made political capital of being berated by Trump, the Italian prime minister had stood out as one of the few Europeans he actually liked: an outspoken conservative at ease talking Maga. But Meloni has also transitioned from a populist...

asia-pacific.title: China-led team develops AI system to track radar-disrupting space hurricanes
asia-pacific.summary:
A China-led team has developed an AI system to detect space hurricanes, a phenomenon in the Earth’s upper atmosphere that can disrupt satellite signals, radar and radio communications.
While the hurricane-like atmospheric phenomenon can have major space weather effects, detection has so far relied on a tedious process of studying satellite images manually.
The team said it had developed a new deep-learning system that could automatically detect and pinpoint space hurricanes through ultraviolet..

asia-pacific.title: Cross-border biotech deals grow more complex as US targets China investment links
asia-pacific.summary:
Cross-border biotech deals between China and the United States are becoming more complicated and could face a modest slowdown as Washington steps up efforts to restrict investment and technology transfers, according to industry analysts.
“There will be more geopolitical scrutiny going forward. This scrutiny may lead to slightly fewer deals than without it,” said Diederik Stadig, senior healthcare economist at ING Research.
Despite the growing regulatory hurdles, Stadig said the broader trend of.

asia-pacific.title: To lead in global education, Hong Kong must go beyond narrow metrics
asia-pacific.summary:
Hong Kong’s universities are often celebrated for their global rankings, research output and international reputation. Yet behind these achievements lies a quieter, more fundamental question: how do we define academic excellence and are we measuring the right things?
Academic life is driven by intrinsic motivations. Scholars are guided by curiosity, a pursuit of excellence and a commitment to professionalism: integrity, responsibility and accountability. Many see their work as part of a larger..

asia-pacific.title: Concerns raised as Hong Kong stops compiling voter turnout data by age and sex
asia-pacific.summary:
Hong Kong election authorities have stopped compiling voter turnout data by age and sex, citing a lack of operational need, in a move that has raised concerns about undermining the transparency of polls.
Analysts and politicians said such statistics were valuable for election hopefuls in understanding voter demographics and formulating policy suggestions, while authorities could use the data to improve governance.
The South China Morning Post asked the Registration and Electoral Office last...

asia-pacific.title: ‘Only death can stop me’: terminally ill Chinese tycoon works 12 hours daily to cure ALS
asia-pacific.summary:
A former Chinese e-commerce executive in the final stages of a rare disease is still working 12-hour days, devoting what time he has left to accelerating the search for a cure.
Cai Lei, 48, a former vice-president of JD.com, one of China’s largest e-commerce companies, has spent seven years battling amyotrophic lateral sclerosis (ALS), an incurable disease that progressively attacks motor neurons.
His condition has recently deteriorated to an advanced stage. Unable to move or speak, Cai can now.

asia-pacific.title: Japan confronts the dark side of its teenagers’ AI addiction
asia-pacific.summary:
She was among five girls in Japan believed to have beaten a boy so severely that he required hospital treatment. Yet her most pressing concern was how much money the group should demand from him. To find an answer, she turned to artificial intelligence.
The alleged assault took place in January in Hachioji, a city in western Greater Tokyo.
For experts, the case has exposed a disturbing pattern: Japanese adolescents instinctively turning to AI to guide their actions, including criminal ones,...

asia-pacific.title: How Singapore’s most notorious mall found God
asia-pacific.summary:
Orchard Towers, a building in Singapore once notorious enough to earn the nickname “Four Floors of Whores”, has found an unlikely new tenant.
On weekends, the site of former nightclubs, illegal massage parlours and at least one murder is now flooded with church-goers.
Cornerstone Community Church officially opened weekly services at the Orchard Road landmark in January after buying six units on the fourth floor – formerly a nightclub – for S$54.5 million (US$42.3 million) in 2025.
Experts say...

asia-pacific.title: British PM Keir Starmer ready to quit, report says
asia-pacific.summary:
Britain’s Observer newspaper said Prime Minister Keir Starmer was expected to resign on Monday and set out a timetable for his departure, though a government source said Starmer remained focused on getting on with the job of governing.
The threat to Starmer’s position, which has been building for ‌months, increased sharply on Friday when his rival Andy Burnham won a seat in parliament that would allow him to launch a formal leadership challenge.
The Observer report said Starmer was discussing...

asia-pacific.title: How ‘lying flat’ went from a niche subculture to mainstream phenomenon
asia-pacific.summary:
In late April, China’s top intelligence agency claimed that foreign forces were weaponising the concept of “lying flat” to dispel young people’s “belief in striving” and undermine social values.
Once a niche subculture, “lying flat” has evolved into a mainstream phenomenon over the past five years.
“Lying flat” became a buzzword on Chinese social media around 2021 during the coronavirus pandemic. At the time, the phrase was usually applied to people who had completely dropped out of the rat...
